name: PlayerAuctions
description: A long-running C2C marketplace for buying and selling in-game digital goods and services (accounts, currency, boosting, coaching) targeting gamers and professional sellers.

details.business: C2C marketplace for digital gaming goods and services
details.targetMarket: Individual gamers who buy/sell game accounts, in-game currency, items, and pay-for-services (boosting, power-leveling, coaching); also professional 'PowerSellers' who operate stores on the platform.

items: PlayerGuardian escrow that holds buyer funds until delivery/confirmation
Buyer service fee (escrow/fraud protection) applied at checkout
Multiple disbursement/payout methods including wire, Skrill, and some crypto options
Seller levels (1–5) based on recent performance to surface higher-trust sellers
Featured seller advertising slots for paid promotion
Dispute resolution and after-sale protection processes
Listings across 300+ games and product categories (accounts, currency, items, services)
Search and filter by game, product type, delivery speed (instant delivery options)
Ratings and reviews on sellers and offers
Free to list, fees charged on completed sales (category-based seller fees)
Automated order handling for certain product types (e.g., account inspection workflows)
Minimum withdrawal limits and disbursement fees depending on payout method

missingFeatures: No built-in recurring subscriptions or booking flow for coaching/ongoing boosting — sellers must manage repeat buyers outside the platform.
Limited identity/verification layer beyond seller-level metrics (no mandatory ID/biometric verification or background checks for high-value sellers), reducing trust for high-ticket transactions.
Complex fee stack (category-specific seller fee + separate disbursement fees + buyer payment gateway fees) with poor in-UI clarity, causing price friction and surprise for sellers.
No public API or B2B integration for game developers, guilds, or partners to automate inventory, pricing, or reporting.

Weak buyer protections for recalled/claimed accounts in some disputes — recurring reviewer complaints indicate the PlayerGuardian coverage and dispute outcomes can be limited or slow for account recalls.

pricingPlans.name: Buyer service fee
pricingPlans.note: Applied per order to cover escrow/fraud protection and platform costs (reduced fee for some crypto payments advertised)

pricingPlans.name: Seller fees (example: accounts)
pricingPlans.note: Category-based fee charged when an offer sells; disbursement/payout method fees are additional and vary by method/country

pricingEvidence.label: Buyer Payments (Help Center)
pricingEvidence.claim: PlayerAuctions applies a buyer service fee of 4.99% of order value + $0.29 (2.99% for some crypto).

pricingEvidence.label: Fees & Disbursement (Help Center)
pricingEvidence.claim: Seller fees are category-based (example: accounts fee examples shown) and additional disbursement fees apply depending on payout method.

companyEvidence.label: About page
companyEvidence.claim: PlayerAuctions states it was founded in 1999 and is operated by PlayerAuctions, Inc.

companyEvidence.label: Crunchbase
companyEvidence.claim: Crunchbase lists PlayerAuctions as based in Los Angeles and notes ItemMania as the owner/network investor.

companyEvidence.label: Datanyze / ZoomInfo company summary
companyEvidence.claim: Third-party profiles report global reach and cite multi-million registered trader counts (3.3M claimed by some directories).

reviewEvidence.label: Trustpilot reviews
reviewEvidence.claim: Thousands of reviews (16k+) with mixed feedback — many positive notes on delivery but recurring complaints about disputes, fees and refunds.

reviewEvidence.label: Reddit discussion (user experiences)
reviewEvidence.claim: Community threads describe PlayerAuctions as generally legitimate but highlight concerns about seller fees and dispute outcomes for recalled accounts.

reviewEvidence.label: BBB / Complaints pages
reviewEvidence.claim: Multiple customer complaints on BBB and complaint boards about account recalls, refunds and slow or unsatisfactory dispute resolution.

featureEvidence.label: How to Buy / PlayerGuardian
featureEvidence.claim: Explains PlayerGuardian escrow protection that holds buyer payments until delivery is confirmed to protect buyers and sellers.

featureEvidence.label: Seller Level help article
featureEvidence.claim: Describes the seller level system (levels 1–5) that promotes reliable sellers based on recent performance metrics.

featureEvidence.label: How to Sell (Help Center)
featureEvidence.claim: Documents workflows for listing offers, delivery inspection for accounts, and the post-sale process.

strengths: Mature escrow/payment infrastructure (PlayerGuardian) that reduces direct buyer-seller fraud and supports automated holds/disbursements.
Extensive game coverage and long track record (founded 1999) with an established brand and large user base, which drives marketplace liquidity.
Seller tools (levels, featured slots, category-specific workflows) that help scale many independent PowerSellers and commercial boosters.
Multiple payout/disbursement options (including some crypto) and automated payout workflows for sellers worldwide.
weaknesses: Frequent public complaints about dispute outcomes and account recalls indicate PlayerGuardian and support can fail for high-value/account-recall cases — a trust risk for buyers of expensive accounts.
Complex and sometimes opaque fee structure (category seller fees + disbursement fees + buyer gateway fees) frustrates sellers and reduces take-home revenue.
Limited modern marketplace features: no public API/B2B integrations, no native recurring bookings/subscriptions for services like coaching, and limited identity verification for high-trust needs.
Support and dispute resolution speed/quality appear inconsistent based on reviews (Trustpilot/BBB/complaint boards), which hurts conversion for new users transacting high-value offers.
